title: Chinese Carrier Liaoning's Taiwan Strait Transit Highlights Intensifying Cross-Strait Military Activity
summary: The Chinese aircraft carrier Liaoning transited the Taiwan Strait in late April, the first carrier passage reported since December, amid a backdrop of near-continuous PLA aerial sorties and cross-median-line flights around Taiwan.
contents:
## Chinese Carrier Liaoning Transits Taiwan Strait

### Transit Details
The aircraft carrier Liaoning sailed through the Taiwan Strait in late April 2026, marking the first Chinese carrier transit reported by Taiwan's Ministry of National Defense since the Fujian made a similar passage in December 2025.

### Regional Military Activity Context
- Taiwan's MND reported monitoring 24 Chinese aircraft sorties, 7 warships, and 1 other vessel in a single 24-hour period
- 11 aircraft crossed the unofficial median line separating Taiwan from mainland China
- Chinese Defense Ministry spokesman Zhang Xiaogang described activities as meant to "safeguard national sovereignty and territorial integrity"
- Japan's guided-missile destroyer JS Ikazuchi conducted its own Taiwan Strait transit days earlier — JMSDF's fourth since June

### Diplomatic Backdrop
The Liaoning transit occurred ahead of the rescheduled Trump-Xi summit planned for May. Taiwan policy remains a central friction point in US-China relations, with Washington continuing to supply arms to Taipei while maintaining its commitment to "a free and open Indo-Pacific."

### Strategic Assessment
Beijing's carrier transits serve as strategic signaling, demonstrating naval capability and asserting sovereignty claims over the entire strait. The increasing frequency of PLA naval and aerial activity around Taiwan suggests a sustained pressure campaign that normalizes high military tempo in the region.

### US Navy Presence
USS George Washington (CVN-73), the only US carrier forward-deployed in the Indo-Pacific, remains in port at Yokosuka, Japan. The Boxer ARG operates near Guam and Saipan.
